Row.Text: +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +3 Palace and +2 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.
+20% [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Wonder Production (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
+1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.

+1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +2 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +4). Specialists also cost 50% less [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
+2 Resource capacity (T3; +6 total).
+2 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +[icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness from Resources.
+1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
+3 Combat Strength in all combat.
+25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold on Fortifications.
+2 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
+2 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.

Suzerain bonuses scale with your [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Pop ([icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]/[icon:YIELD_CULTURE]/[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION]/[icon:YIELD_GOLD]/[icon:YIELD_FOOD]), or +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.
+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +3 Palace and +2 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.
+25% [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Wonder Production (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
+1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.

+1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +2 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +4). Specialists also cost 50% less [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
+2 Resource capacity (T3; +6 total).
+2 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +[icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness from Resources.
+1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
+3 Combat Strength in all combat.
+25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold on Fortifications.
+2 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
+2 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.
+2 Great Work slots on Temples (Relics, Codices, Artifacts, Great Works), +2 [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness per Building, and +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per Great Work in this city (T2).

Suzerain bonuses scale with your [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Pop ([icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]/[icon:YIELD_CULTURE]/[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION]/[icon:YIELD_GOLD]/[icon:YIELD_FOOD]), or +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.
+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +4 Palace and +3 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.
+30% [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Wonder Production (T2).
+1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
+1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
While Joyous or happier: +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture per 4 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop, increasing further while Ecstatic.

+1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +3 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +6). Specialists also cost 50% less [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food and [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
+3 Resource capacity (T3; +9 total).
+3 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +[icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS] Happiness from Resources.
+1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per 2 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Urban Pop (T3).
+3 Combat Strength in all combat.
+25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold on Fortifications.
+2 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
+2 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.

Suzerain bonuses scale with your [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Pop ([icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]/[icon:YIELD_CULTURE]/[icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION]/[icon:YIELD_GOLD]/[icon:YIELD_FOOD]), or +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.

Replace.Text: +1[icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]Science on Brickyards and Harbors. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1[icon:YIELD_CULTURE]Culture on Saw Pits and Fishing Quays. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:WAR_SUPPORT]War Support. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1[icon:YIELD_GOLD]Gold on Saw Pits and Harbors. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD]Food on Granaries and Fishing Quays. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS]Happiness on Granaries and Brickyards. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), you also earn +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per turn for every city-state you are Suzerain of - compounding your diplomacy.

+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]Science on Brickyards, Harbors, and Stonecutters. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE]Culture on Saw Pits, Fishing Quays, and Stonecutters. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:WAR_SUPPORT]War Support. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD]Gold on Saw Pits, Harbors, and Sawmills. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD]Food on Granaries, Fishing Quays, and Gristmills. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS]Happiness on Granaries, Brickyards, and Gristmills. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), you also earn +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per turn for every city-state you are Suzerain of - compounding your diplomacy.

+1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE]Science on Brickyards, Harbors, Stonecutters, and Ironworks. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_SCIENCE] Science for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE]Culture on Saw Pits, Fishing Quays, Stonecutters, and Grocers. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_CULTURE] Culture for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:WAR_SUPPORT]War Support. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_PRODUCTION] Production for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD]Gold on Saw Pits, Harbors, Sawmills, and Ironworks. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations.[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_GOLD] Gold for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD]Food on Granaries, Brickyards, Gristmills, and Grocers. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), each of your cities also earns +1 [icon:YIELD_FOOD] Food for every 3 [icon:YIELD_POPULATION] Population it has - a dense city makes this Suzerain bonus grow with it.

+1 [icon:YIELD_HAPPINESS]Happiness on Granaries, Brickyards, Gristmills, and Grocers. This bonus can be chosen by multiple Civilizations[N][N]Metropolis Ascendant: while you stay tall (one city per hemisphere), you also earn +2 [icon:YIELD_DIPLOMACY] Influence per turn for every city-state you are Suzerain of - compounding your diplomacy.
